---
title: "Temperature Sensor Analysis Guide"
author: "Marc Los Huertos"
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Set-Up Directories

project/
├── data_raw/          # raw sensor CSVs (many files)
│   ├── 260191640D.csv
│   ├── 260191641E.csv
│   └── ...
├── data_meta/
│   └── locations_treatments.csv   # NOT created yet
├── data_processed/
└── scripts/
    └── read_and_merge.R


## Set-Up Sensor Metadata

```{r metadata}

```

## Merge Data

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
# --------------------------------------------------
# Libraries
# --------------------------------------------------
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Paths
# --------------------------------------------------
raw_data_dir  <- "data_raw"
meta_file     <- "data_meta/locations_treatments.csv"

# --------------------------------------------------
# Function: read ONE sensor file
# --------------------------------------------------
read_sensor_file <- function(file_path) {

  location_id <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file_path))

  read_tsv(
    file_path,
    skip = "MM/DD/YY",
    show_col_types = FALSE,
    trim_ws = TRUE
  ) |>
    clean_names() |>
    mutate(
      location_id = as.character(location_id),
      datetime = as.POSIXct(
        paste(mm_dd_yy, hh_mm_ss),
        format = "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S",
        tz = "UTC"
      )
    ) |>
    select(
      location_id,
      datetime,
      fahrenheit,
      centigrade,
      status
    )
}


# --------------------------------------------------
# Read ALL sensor files
# --------------------------------------------------
sensor_files <- list.files(
  raw_data_dir,
  pattern = "\\.csv$",
  full.names = TRUE
)

sensor_data <- sensor_files |>
  map_dfr(read_sensor_file)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Read location / treatment metadata
# (will fail loudly if missing — intentional)
# --------------------------------------------------
locations <- read_csv(meta_file, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names()

# Expected columns:
# location_id | site | treatment (shade / sun)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Merge sensor data with metadata
# --------------------------------------------------
sensor_merged <- sensor_data |>
  left_join(locations, by = "location_id")

# --------------------------------------------------
# Basic integrity checks
# --------------------------------------------------
# 1. Any locations missing treatment?
missing_treatment <- sensor_merged |>
  filter(is.na(treatment)) |>
  distinct(location_id)

if (nrow(missing_treatment) > 0) {
  warning(
    "The following location_ids are missing treatment assignments:\n",
    paste(missing_treatment$location_id, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# --------------------------------------------------
# Save processed data
# --------------------------------------------------
write_csv(
  sensor_merged,
  "data_processed/sensor_data_with_treatments.csv"
)
``
```

## Group Data

```{r}
sensor_merged |>
  group_by(treatment) |>
  summarise(mean_temp = mean(temperature, na.rm = TRUE))
```

## Analyze Data

```{r}
lme4::lmer(temp ~ treatment + (1 | location_id), data = sensor_merged)
```
